summary: Groove‑heavy show with superb 'When I Paint My Masterpiece' & classic Brokedown Palace
blurb: The first set rolls from Mississippi Half-Step into a standout When I Paint My Masterpiece and solid rockers like The Promised Land. The second set stays deep in the groove, highlighted by an extended The Other One that feeds into a joyous Good Lovin'. The encore caps it off with Touch of Grey and a classic Brokedown Palace.
review: On August 16, 1987 at Town Park in Telluride the Dead delivered a tight, post‑coma set that feels both intimate and groove‑driven. Jerry handled the high altitude beautifully, laying down flawless guitar work on the highlighted When I Paint My Masterpiece while Brent’s tasteful keys and the dual drumming of Mickey and Bill kept the rhythm solid throughout. Phil anchored the band with his steady bass lines, and Bob added his characteristic rhythmic chime, especially during the rolling jam on The Other One that leads into a jubilant Good Lovin'. The encore was a perfect close—Touch of Grey’s sing‑along optimism followed by a classic Brokedown Palace moment that many fans still cite as a highlight of this summer run. Despite some track‑order quirks in certain matrix releases, the performance itself remains a strong example of the Dead’s late‑’80s groove and songcraft.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two out of three recordings praise the show with terms like 'superb', 'classic', and note standout songs; only one mixed comment mentions ordering issues, not performance quality. Consistent positive language yields a moderate‑high sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: FM source with clear sound and complete set, widely regarded as the most reliable capture of this performance.

key_highlights: Superb rendition of 'When I Paint My Masterpiece' that shines throughout the show
Classic Brokedown Palace moment to close the encore
Deep groove on second‑set jams, especially The Other One leading into Good Lovin'
Solid overall energy despite minor track‑order issues in some releases

band_performance.Jerry: Handled the high altitude great and delivered flawless guitar work, especially on When I Paint My Masterpiece.
band_performance.Phil: Provided a steady bass foundation that kept the groove locked in throughout both sets.
band_performance.Bob: Added solid rhythm guitar that complemented the jam sections and supported the overall flow.
band_performance.Brent: Delivered tasteful keyboard textures that enhanced the song dynamics without overpowering the band.
band_performance.Mickey: Kept tight, complementary percussion that helped drive the deep second‑set groove.
band_performance.Billy: Locked in with Mickey, offering a steady dual‑drum backbone especially during The Other One and Good Lovin'.
